events.title: OCFA responds to toxic chemical leak at GKN Aerospace
events.description: OCFA crews and Garden Grove PD respond to reports of toxic chemical leak at GKN Aerospace Transparency Systems, 12122 Western Ave, Garden Grove.

events.title: 34,000-gallon MMA tank confirmed overheated and off-gassing vapor
events.description: OCFA confirms 34,000-gallon tank containing ~7,000 gallons of methyl methacrylate (MMA) has overheated and is off-gassing vapor. Relief valve and sprinkler system activated automatically.

events.title: OCFA Captain Doran briefs media on overheated industrial tank
events.description: OCFA Captain Sean Doran briefs media: "We arrived to a 34,000-gallon tank containing an industrial chemical that had been overheated inside the vessel." Tank's sprinkler system activated as designed.

events.title: Initial evacuation orders issued for areas around GKN facility
events.description: Initial evacuation orders issued for areas north of Garden Grove Blvd, east of Monarch St, south of Orangewood Ave, west of Beach Blvd.

events.title: Day 1 evacuation orders lifted after cooling operations
events.description: Evacuation orders lifted after crews spend hours cooling tank with water hoses and reducing vapor production.

events.title: OCFA reissues evacuation orders due to changing conditions
events.description: OCFA reissues evacuation orders "due to changing conditions." First mention of tank possibly rupturing or exploding.

events.title: Initial Day 2 evacuation zone defined
events.description: Initial evacuation zone: north of Garden Grove Blvd, east of Springdale Ave, west of Dale St, south of Orangewood Ave.

events.title: Tank temperature reads 77°F; cooling operations begin
events.description: Tank temperature read at 77°F in the morning; responders begin water-cooling operations.

events.title: Evacuation zone expanded to 1-mile buffer, ~40,000 people affected
events.description: Evacuation zone expanded to north of Trask Ave, south of Ball Rd, east of Valley View St, west of Dale St — creating ~1-mile buffer around tank. ~40,000 people affected.

events.title: OCFA Chief Covey warns of only two outcomes: spill or explosion
events.description: OCFA Division Chief Craig Covey afternoon briefing: tank is "actively in crisis." Warns of only two outcomes — chemical spill or thermal runaway explosion. "There are literally two options left remaining."

events.title: Faulty valve identified as core problem preventing tank neutralization
events.description: Faulty valve identified as core problem — valve seized due to chemical reaction, preventing neutralization or draining of tank contents.

events.title: ~15% of evacuees (~6,000 people) refusing to leave
events.description: Garden Grove Police Chief Amir El-Farra reports ~15% of evacuees (~6,000 people) refusing to leave.

events.title: Over a dozen schools close; GGUSD announces closures
events.description: Over a dozen schools temporarily close; schools adjacent to evacuation area cancel outdoor activities. GGUSD announces closures.

events.title: OC District Attorney opens investigation; anonymous tip line launched
events.description: Orange County District Attorney Todd Spitzer begins investigation; investigators survey site via drones. Orders GKN Aerospace to preserve records. Anonymous tip line opened (714-347-8714).

events.title: Night team reads tank at 90°F — higher than drone readings indicated
events.description: Night team approaches neighboring tank for neutralization attempt; reads internal temperature gauge at 90°F — significantly higher than 77°F morning reading. External drone readings had been misleading.

events.title: NPR and CNN pick up story nationally
events.description: NPR picks up story nationally. CNN begins live coverage.

events.title: OCFA morning briefing: tank continued heating despite stabilization signs
events.description: OCFA morning briefing: tank continued to heat internally despite earlier signs of stabilization. Incident Commander Covey says cooling efforts based on external drone readings "proved incorrect."

events.title: Tank temperature rising at ~1°F per hour
events.description: Temperature rising at ~1°F per hour per OCFA Incident Commander Covey.

events.title: Evacuation expanded to ~50,000 people across 9 square miles
events.description: Evacuation expanded to ~50,000 people across 9 square miles — including parts of Anaheim, Buena Park, Cypress, Stanton, and Westminster.

events.title: Blast zone and impact zone maps released; health effects described
events.description: OCFA Division Chief Nick Freeman and OC Health Officer Dr. Regina Chinsio-Kwong release blast zone and impact zone maps. Dr. Chinsio-Kwong warns MMA can cause respiratory issues, itching/burning eyes, nausea, headaches.

events.title: Covey warns Friday's positive indications may have been false flags
events.description: OCFA Division Chief Covey releases 5-minute critical incident briefing: positive indications from Friday's investigation "may have been false flags."

events.title: First responders pulled back; remote water spraying continues
events.description: First responders pulled back from immediate area around tank for safety; continue remotely spraying water.

events.title: Containment channels built to protect storm drains if tank ruptures
events.description: Responders construct channels and containment areas to prevent liquid MMA from flowing into storm drains, waterways, or ocean if tank ruptures.

events.title: Governor Newsom declares state of emergency in Orange County
events.description: Governor Gavin Newsom declares state of emergency in Orange County. Directs Cal OES and all state agencies to support response. Makes state-owned properties and fairgrounds available for sheltering.

events.title: Potential crack discovered in tank; may be relieving pressure
events.description: OCFA Interim Fire Chief TJ McGovern reports potential crack discovered in tank. No liquid leaking through crack. Crack may be relieving internal pressure, potentially preventing explosion.

events.title: Newsom requests federal emergency declaration from President Trump
events.description: Newsom requests President Trump issue federal emergency declaration. Senators Padilla and Schiff, Rep. Derek Tran send letters urging approval.

events.title: Class-action lawsuit filed against GKN Aerospace
events.description: Class-action lawsuit filed against GKN Aerospace by X-Law Group P.C. and Presidio Law Firm on behalf of two Garden Grove residents. Seeks damages in hundreds of millions or billions.

events.title: Covey: "This is as real as it gets. Worst-case scenario I've ever faced."
events.description: OCFA Division Chief Craig Covey: "This is as real as it gets. It's the worst-case scenario I've ever faced in my career."

events.title: Evacuation shelters opened across Orange County
events.description: Evacuation shelters opened: JFK High School (La Palma), Savanna High School (Anaheim), Oceanview High School (Huntington Beach), Mile Square Regional Park Freedom Hall (Fountain Valley), Los Amigos High School (Fountain Valley), Golden West College (Huntington Beach). OC Fair & Event Center (Costa Mesa) opens for RV parking.

events.title: CA Attorney General urges evacuees to report price gouging
events.description: California Attorney General Rob Bonta urges evacuees to report price gouging.

events.title: International media coverage begins; Vietnamese-language stations translate updates
events.description: News18 (India) begins live international coverage. ABC News national runs "What to know 48 hours in" segment. Local Vietnamese radio and TV stations begin translating emergency updates for non-English-speaking residents. Garden Grove city website posts alerts in Vietnamese, Korean, and Spanish.

events.title: OCFA announces all-night operation to assess tank pressure relief
events.description: OCFA announces all-night operation to determine whether excess tank pressure has been relieved.

events.title: Tank temperature reaches 100°F — maxing out gauge reading limit
events.description: Tank temperature reaches at least 100°F — maxing out gauge's reading limit.

events.title: GKN team begins removing external tank insulation to aid cooling
events.description: GKN Aerospace team works with OCFA to remove external insulation from tank to help cool chemicals inside.

events.title: 785 state and local first responders deployed to Orange County
events.description: Governor Newsom's office reports 785 state and local first responders deployed to Orange County.

events.title: 24 air quality monitors deployed; readings completely normal
events.description: South Coast AQMD and US EPA confirm 24 air quality monitors deployed around evacuation zone; air quality levels remain "completely normal." EPA dispatches two on-scene coordinators.

events.title: EPA Administrator Zeldin: "Tank will fail" but likely low-volume release
events.description: EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin tells CNN: "The most likely scenario is one of a low-volume release, where the local authorities are going to be able to monitor, neutralize, and contain the threat." Confirms "the tank will fail."

events.title: FEMA deploys liaison; activates atmospheric assessment center
events.description: FEMA deploys Liaison Officer and team to State Emergency Operations Center. Activates Interagency Modeling and Atmospheric Assessment Center for plume modeling. White House confirms EPA has air monitoring at 20 locations.

events.title: Temporary shelters reach full capacity for overnight stays
events.description: Orange County officials report temporary shelters at full capacity for overnight stays.

events.title: Joint operation successfully removes insulation; crack confirmed relieving pressure
events.description: Technical specialists conduct joint operation with OCFA to assess tank more closely; successfully remove external insulation material. During operation, identify crack relieving pressure.

events.title: 12 GGUSD campuses confirmed closed through May 27
events.description: 12 GGUSD campuses confirmed closed through May 27 (final school day): Barker, Bryant, Carver, Enders, Garden Park, Lawrence, Wakeham, Patton (elementary); Bell, Alamitos (intermediate); Pacifica, Rancho Alamitos (high school). Distance learning for final 2 instructional days, no penalties.

events.title: Multiple school districts beyond GGUSD announce closures
events.description: Westminster SD closes Fryberger, Sequoia and other schools; Magnolia SD closes Pyles STEM Academy; Savanna SD closes all campuses for week of May 25. Cypress SD gives pickup option for Vessels and Morris Elementary.

events.title: Uber, gyms, and World Central Kitchen offer support to evacuees
events.description: Uber offers free rides up to $40 to shelters. Planet Fitness, 24 Hour Fitness, LA Fitness open all OC locations to evacuees. World Central Kitchen provides meals alongside local food trucks.

events.title: Vietnamese community opens private homes; Dieu Ngu Pagoda shelters evacuees
events.description: Vietnamese community members open private homes to evacuees; A Huntington Beach resident shelters 40 people in vacant family properties. Dieu Ngu Pagoda (Westminster) opens for shelter with vegetarian meals.

events.title: TIME Magazine and Fox News publish national features on crisis
events.description: TIME Magazine publishes feature: "Garden Grove: Tens of Thousands Ordered to Evacuate Over Chemical Tank Explosion Risk." Fox News national publishes: "Orange County chemical tank 'actively in crisis' could explode or spill."

events.title: LA Mayor confirms no risk to Los Angeles; neighboring cities monitor
events.description: LA Mayor confirms no risk to city of Los Angeles. Cities of Lakewood, Long Beach, and Hawaiian Gardens monitor situation. LAFD confirms no risk.

events.title: Tank crack verified overnight; pressure releasing through crack
events.description: OCFA Interim Fire Chief TJ McGovern confirms crack in tank verified overnight; pressure releasing through crack.

events.title: BLEVE threat eliminated; tank temperature dropping from 100°F to 93°F
events.description: OCFA morning press conference: BLEVE threat "is now off the table." Interim Chief McGovern and Incident Commander Covey confirm crack has relieved tank pressure. Tank temperature dropped from ~100°F to 93°F and continuing to decline.

events.title: McGovern cautions: smaller explosion or leak still possible
events.description: McGovern cautions: "Crisis is not averted. We still have potential for an explosion that is not as bad as a BLEVE, but it is still a threat." Smaller explosion or leak still possible. No active leak currently.

events.title: Only 1 known crack confirmed; liquid MMA status being assessed
events.description: OCFA clarifies only 1 known crack in tank. OCFA PIO Nick Garton: "We're trying to confirm that there's still a liquid form of this chemical that could potentially leak out."

events.title: Firefighters can now enter tank area at reduced risk during daytime
events.description: Elimination of BLEVE threat allows firefighters to enter tank area at reduced risk during daytime hours.

events.title: President Trump signs federal emergency declaration for Orange County
events.description: President Trump signs federal emergency declaration for Orange County, per OC Supervisor Janet Nguyen and Rep. Derek Tran. Authorizes FEMA to provide direct federal assistance.

events.title: 50,000 residents remain under unchanged evacuation orders
events.description: 50,000 residents remain under evacuation orders. Evacuation zone unchanged.

events.title: Air quality remains normal across all 24 monitoring stations
events.description: Air quality levels continue to read normal across all 24 SCAQMD/EPA monitoring stations around evacuation zone.

events.title: Evacuation zone reduced 65%; 34,000 residents can return home
events.description: Evacuation zone reduced from ~50,000 to ~16,000 residents. New boundaries: Orangewood Ave (north), Dale St (east), Knott St (west), Garden Grove Blvd (south). Approximately 65% of impacted residents can return home.

events.title: Overnight operation removes tank insulation to improve cooling
events.description: Firefighters removed weather protection and insulation from the outside of the tank overnight to help water cooling be more efficient at reducing temperature.

events.title: OCFA confirms no threat of catastrophic explosion; tank stabilizing
events.description: OCFA confirms there is no longer a threat of a catastrophic explosion. Tank temperature continuing to decrease. Ongoing atmospheric monitoring confirms no chemicals leaking from the crack.

events.title: McGovern: worst-case scenario resolved, but incident not over
events.description: OCFA Interim Chief McGovern at Monday afternoon press conference: "The most catastrophic and worst-case scenario has been mitigated and resolved, but it's not over yet." No injuries to civilians or firefighters throughout the incident. OCFA Incident Commander Covey: "The liquid potential in the tank [has been] greatly reduced, as it has been hardened."

events.title: 7 arrests during evacuation; millions of gallons of water used to cool tank
events.description: At the Day 6 evening press conference, Garden Grove Police Chief Amir El-Farra reports 7 total arrests since the incident began: 1 for looting and 6 for prowling; DA's office to prosecute to fullest extent. OCFA Incident Commander Craig Covey reveals scale of cooling operations: a deluge system plus a ground monitor flowing 1,250 gallons per minute for five consecutive days — totaling millions of gallons of water used.

events.title: Tank temperature steady at 92°F; main cooling hose stream deactivated
events.description: OCFA Fire Capt. and PIO Greg Barta reports tank temperature holding at a steady 92°F, down from 100°F peak. Officials deactivate one of the main unmanned master hose streams that had been continuously spraying water on the tank for days. Onsite sprinkler system remains active. MMA continues to harden inside tank, further reducing spill threat.

events.title: Crews begin transferring MMA from third unaffected tank into neutralizing-agent tank
events.description: Officials announce crews are moving product from the third, unaffected GKN Aerospace tank into a second tank that already contains a neutralizing agent — creating the conditions to eventually neutralize and isolate the MMA from the compromised primary tank. The primary tank's seized valves continue to prevent direct offloading of its contents.

events.title: Garden Grove City Council holds special meeting on tank crisis
events.description: City of Garden Grove holds special City Council meeting focused on the incident, the city's response, and the latest information available.

events.title: McGovern: water cooling to be shut down system by system to test tank stability
events.description: OCFA Interim Fire Chief TJ McGovern announces at Tuesday morning briefing that crews will begin gradually shutting down the two water-cooling systems to test whether the tank can stabilize on its own. "We're going to shut down one of those two systems, and we're going to monitor the temperature. We're looking for it to stabilize. We don't want it to come up. We're hoping it comes down. If that looks good throughout the day, then we're going to shut down the second system, and we are going to watch that." Tank temperature holding steady at 92°F.

events.title: DA Spitzer expands criminal investigation; adds price-gouging probe
events.description: Orange County District Attorney Todd Spitzer announces expansion of the criminal investigation into GKN Aerospace. The scope now includes alleged price gouging by third parties and predatory solicitation practices by attorneys targeting evacuees. Spitzer: he wants to make sure anyone impacted, including tens of thousands forced from their homes, is made whole. His office is looking into price gouging to prevent illicit activity.

events.title: All water cooling deactivated; crews walk and climb tank as MMA solidifies
events.description: AIR7 aerial video above GKN Aerospace shows no water being sprayed on the tank. Both cooling systems shut down; crews observed walking around the tank and climbing on top. Focus shifts from explosion prevention to assessing how to safely access the tank interior. MMA believed to be solidifying inside. OCFA Chief McGovern: "We gotta get the fire problem handled first, stabilize that temperature, and then the team will make that determination — now, how do we get inside?" 16,000 residents remain under reduced evacuation orders.

events.title: Residents demand GKN accountability at Garden Grove City Council meeting
events.description: At a second special Tuesday evening City Council meeting, OCFA Incident Commander Craig Covey and Garden Grove Police Chief Amir El-Farra break the news that evacuation orders will be lifted. Angry residents demand accountability from city leaders and GKN Aerospace, questioning why a company handling such chemicals operates so close to homes, schools, and businesses. Garden Grove Mayor Stephanie Klopfenstein pledges GKN will be held accountable: "Our community deserves to know what happened, why it happened and whether laws or regulations were violated, and what steps we must take to prevent something like this from ever happening again."

events.title: All evacuation orders lifted; OCFA, county health, and EPA declare no risk
events.description: OCFA announces all evacuation orders lifted at 7:30 PM PDT for the final 16,000 remaining evacuees — ending a 7-day crisis that displaced up to 50,000 residents. County health officials, OCFA fire crews, and the EPA jointly confirmed no chemical leak, no threat of explosion, no threat of fire, and no risk to the public from the damaged GKN Aerospace tank. EPA on-scene coordinator Chris Myers: "No exceedances have been detected at any time throughout this event. There was no contamination. There were no fumes. There were no vapors that came from this incident." No injuries were reported during the entire 7-day incident.

events.title: Residents return home; community demands GKN accountability
events.description: Following the 7:30 PM PDT all-clear, tens of thousands of residents returned home after the 7-day evacuation. Many expressed relief but also anger over the week-long displacement. Rep. Derek Tran (CA-45): "While immediate danger has been lifted, residents are still left to pick up the pieces. Our community is rightfully angry, and I will continue working around the clock to demand accountability from all responsible actors." NBC LA reported the evacuation order for all Garden Grove residents was officially lifted by the Garden Grove Police Department. GKN Aerospace's prior $909,935.95 SCAQMD settlement from early 2025 — stemming from air quality violations first cited in a 2020 inspection — drew renewed scrutiny as residents demanded answers about how the company was permitted to store hazardous chemicals so close to homes and schools.

events.title: GGUSD announces all 12 closed campuses reopen Wednesday — last day of school year
events.description: Garden Grove Unified School District announces all schools will reopen Wednesday, May 28 — the final day of the school year. Students will not be penalized if unable to attend as families continue to recover; students held harmless on grades. The announcement follows the 7:30 PM PDT all-clear. Hazmat crews remain on Western Ave treating remaining MMA within a 300-foot exclusion zone around the GKN tanks.

events.title: Evacuation shelters formally close; SBDC business recovery webinar announced
events.description: Orange County officials announce formal closure of four evacuation shelters opened during the 7-day crisis, including Golden West College (Huntington Beach), Los Amigos High School (Fountain Valley), and Los Alamitos High School. Ongoing support services and recovery resources remain available. The Small Business Development Center (SBDC) announces a free webinar for businesses impacted by the chemical incident, covering available federal, state, county, and local resources, business support services, and guidance. Western Ave remains closed between Chapman Ave and Garden Grove Blvd with a 300-ft exclusion zone as hazmat crews continue treating solidified MMA around the GKN tanks.

events.title: Former GKN employee raises prior safety concerns; urges use of right-to-know laws
events.description: Valerie Morales, a former environmental health and safety manager who spent 18 years in the field including time at GKN Aerospace's Santa Ana facility, speaks to Fox 11 Los Angeles about the crisis. Morales calls the incident a wake-up call and urges California residents to use existing "right-to-know" laws to research hazardous materials stored in facilities near their homes. Her comments come as the Orange County DA's criminal investigation continues and multiple class-action lawsuits are filed against GKN Aerospace.

events.title: GKN Aerospace SVP Steve Carlin issues formal apology to displaced community
events.description: GKN Aerospace Senior Vice President Steve Carlin issues a formal public apology for the 7-day chemical tank crisis: "On behalf of the team at GKN Aerospace, I want to say how sorry we are for the uncertainty and disruption this situation has caused. The past several days have been difficult for residents, businesses, and the broader Garden Grove, Stanton, and surrounding Orange County communities. We are relieved that the incident has been resolved and residents have been able to return home." The statement comes as hundreds of angry residents demand accountability and the permanent closure of the GKN facility, and as the Orange County DA's criminal investigation, multiple class-action lawsuits, and community pressure continue.

events.title: 37+ lawsuits filed against GKN Aerospace in OC and federal courts
events.description: As of Day 8, 29 claims against GKN Aerospace have been filed in Orange County Superior Court and approximately 8 in federal court — beyond the initial class-action filed May 23 — as residents and attorneys continue to seek damages for the 7-day displacement of up to 50,000 people. Separately, a coalition of community and advocacy groups is calling for permanent closure of the GKN Garden Grove facility. The Orange County DA criminal investigation remains open.

events.title: Community recovery meeting at Cal State Fullerton for evacuees
events.description: A community recovery meeting is held at California State University Fullerton for residents displaced by the 7-day chemical emergency. Consumer protection experts are available, and OCFA Interim Fire Chief TJ McGovern attends to brief the public. The meeting runs until approximately 5:30 PM PDT, after which many residents head to a Garden Grove City Council session to continue demanding accountability from GKN Aerospace and city leadership.

events.title: All roads around GKN Aerospace fully reopen; 300-ft exclusion zone only remains
events.description: OCFA confirms all roads closed during the 7-day chemical emergency — including Western Ave between Chapman Ave and Garden Grove Blvd — have fully reopened. The only restriction remaining is a 300-foot exclusion zone immediately surrounding the GKN Aerospace tanks, where hazmat crews continue treating and monitoring the solidified MMA. The Orange County DA criminal investigation and multiple class-action lawsuits remain ongoing.

events.title: City launches community impact survey; OC United Way opens recovery fund
events.description: The City of Garden Grove launches an online community impact survey asking residents and businesses to document the effects of the evacuation and chemical emergency (ggcity.org/hazmat-incident/survey). Separately, Orange County United Way launches the OC Community Resilience Fund to provide direct financial support for residents and businesses in Garden Grove, Stanton, Westminster, Cypress, Anaheim, and Buena Park affected by the incident.

events.title: Residents publicly call for permanent GKN Aerospace plant closure
events.description: KTLA reports hundreds of Garden Grove residents are publicly demanding the permanent closure of the GKN Aerospace facility at 12122 Western Ave. Community members question how a company handling explosive industrial chemicals was permitted to operate within a mile of homes and schools. The City Council took no action at the special meeting despite chants and pleas from residents. The Orange County DA criminal investigation, multiple class-action lawsuits, and prior SCAQMD settlement history continue to fuel community pressure.

events.title: Mass tort against GKN Aerospace surpasses 100 plaintiffs; multiple class-actions filed
events.description: More than 100 people have joined a mass tort lawsuit against GKN Aerospace following the 7-day chemical emergency. Beyond the initial X-Law Group class action (filed May 23), DiCello Levitt LLP filed a separate federal class action on May 27 on behalf of evacuated property owners and renters, and a second federal case (Sanchez v. GKN Aerospace Transparency Systems, Case No. 8:26-cv-01296, C.D. Cal.) was filed May 26. Westminster, Anaheim, and the Orange County Board of Supervisors ratified local emergencies at their May 28 meetings, a step officials say is necessary to pursue state and federal reimbursement for displaced residents and affected businesses. The Orange County DA criminal investigation remains open.

events.title: OC Board Chair Chaffee calls on GKN to set up compensation claims process
events.description: Orange County Board of Supervisors Chair Doug Chaffee publicly calls on GKN Aerospace to establish a compensation and claims process for the approximately 50,000 displaced residents and affected businesses. "We encourage them to set up a claims process and acknowledge responsibility. We're looking for that to happen, so the public can be reimbursed." Chaffee says GKN should compensate businesses forced to shut down, residents who lost wages, and those who incurred hotel and displacement expenses. Details on how and when reimbursement would occur remain unclear, with county officials still exploring options. OC County has filed for FEMA reimbursement for government response costs, while pushing GKN to pay for private losses.
